item.title: Python 简介 | Python Intro
item.description:
它由 Guido van Rossum 创建，从此成为世界上最流行的编程语言之一。

它拥有庞大的标准库和蓬勃发展的第三方库和工具生态系统，这使其适用于广泛的应用程序，包括 Web 开发、数据分析、科学计算、人工智能等。

这有助于使代码更具可读性和更易于理解。

这可能使它比 C 或 C++ 等编译语言慢，但也使其更灵活且更易于使用。

总的来说，Python 是一种功能强大且用途广泛的语言，广泛应用于许多不同的行业和领域。

以下是 Python 最适合和广泛使用的一些领域：

这些框架提供了许多有用的功能和库，可以轻松快速高效地构建复杂的 Web 应用程序。

数据科学和机器学习：由于 NumPy、Pandas 和 Scikit-learn 等强大库的可用性，Python 广泛用于数据科学和机器学习应用程序。 这些库提供了大量用于数据操作、分析和建模的工具，使 Python 成为数据科学家和机器学习工程师的热门选择。

科学计算：由于 SciPy、Matplotlib 和 IPython 等库，Python 也广泛用于科学计算应用程序。 这些库为科学计算提供了许多有用的工具，例如数值积分、优化和绘图。

自动化和脚本：Python 是一种用于自动化和脚本任务的优秀语言，这要归功于它的简单性和易用性。 它通常用于网络抓取、文件处理和系统管理等任务。

教育：由于其易用性和简单性，Python 作为第一门编程语言被广泛用于教育领域。 许多大学和学校现在将 Python 作为计算机科学课程的一部分进行教授。

总的来说，Python 是一门通用的语言，可用于各种应用程序，使其成为许多行业和领域开发人员的热门选择。

学习python需要多长时间？？

学习 Python 所需的时间长短取决于多种因素，例如您以前的编程经验、您可以投入学习的时间以及您想要达到的熟练程度。 但是，这里有一些一般准则：

基本能力：如果你想学习 Python 的基础知识并能够编写简单的脚本和程序，可能需要几周到几个月的持续练习。

中级熟练度：如果你想能够编写更复杂的程序并使用Python进行数据分析或网页开发，可能需要几个月到一年的持续练习。

高级熟练度：如果你想成为 Python 专家并能够为开源项目做出贡献或开发自己的库和框架，可能需要几年的持续实践和学习。

请记住，学习 Python 是一个持续的过程，即使是经验丰富的程序员也会继续学习有关该语言及其生态系统的新知识。 最重要的是开始持续练习和学习，并专注于构建项目和解决问题以巩固您的知识。

蟒蛇基础

以下是 Python 编程语言的一些基础知识：

语法：Python 的语法非常简洁明了，代码易于阅读，没有分号和大括号。 变量：在 Python 中，变量用于存储值。 与其他编程语言不同，变量不需要声明为特定数据类型。

数据类型：Python 有多种内置数据类型，包括字符串、整数、浮点数和布尔值。 它还支持更复杂的数据类型，例如列表、字典和元组。

控制结构：Python 有多种控制结构，包括 if/else 语句、循环（for 和 while）以及用于处理错误的 try/except 块。

函数：函数在 Python 中用于组织和重用代码。 它们是使用“def”关键字定义的，可以接受参数和返回值。

库和模块：Python 有一个庞大的标准库，以及一个庞大的第三方库和模块生态系统，可以将它们导入您的代码中以提供额外的功能。

面向对象编程：Python 是一种面向对象的编程语言，这意味着它支持对象和类的创建和使用。

这些只是 Python 编程的一些基础知识。 学习基础知识对于为 Python 中更高级的编程概念打下坚实的基础至关重要。

在 Windows 机器上使用 Python 的先决条件

要在 Windows 机器上运行 Python，您需要满足以下先决条件：

安装 Python：第一步是从 Python 官方网站下载并安装 Python 解释器。 您可以选择 Python 2.x 或 Python 3.x，但建议使用 Python 3.x，因为它是最新版本。

设置环境变量：安装 Python 后，需要设置环境变量。 这将确保您的计算机知道在哪里可以找到 Python 解释器。 要设置环境变量，您需要按照以下步骤操作：

右键单击“此电脑”并选择“属性”。 单击“高级系统设置”，然后单击“环境变量”。 在“系统变量”下，单击“新建”并输入“PYTHON_HOME”作为变量名，输入Python 安装位置作为变量值（例如，C:\Python39）。 编辑“路径”变量并将“%PYTHON_HOME%\Scripts”和“%PYTHON_HOME%”添加到路径列表中。

安装代码编辑器：要编写和运行 Python 代码，您需要一个代码编辑器。 Some popular options for Python include PyCharm, VS Code, and Spyder. Python 的一些流行选项包括 PyCharm、VS Code 和 Spyder。



原文:

It was created by Guido van Rossum, and it has since become one of the most popular programming languages in the world.

Python is known for its simplicity and readability, which makes it an excellent language for beginners to learn. It has a large standard library and a thriving ecosystem of third-party libraries and tools, which makes it useful for a wide range of applications, including web development, data analysis, scientific computing, artificial intelligence, and more.

One of the key features of Python is its use of indentation to delimit blocks of code, rather than using curly braces or other similar syntax. This helps to make the code more readable and easier to understand.

Python is an interpreted language, which means that code is executed directly by the interpreter, rather than being compiled into machine code. This can make it slower than compiled languages like C or C++, but it also makes it more flexible and easier to work with.

Overall, Python is a powerful and versatile language that is widely used in many different industries and fields.

Where Python is best suited and widely used??

Python is a versatile language that can be used in a wide variety of applications. Here are some of the areas where Python is best suited and widely used:

Web Development: Python is a popular choice for building web applications, especially with the Django and Flask web frameworks. These frameworks provide a lot of useful features and libraries that make it easy to build complex web applications quickly and efficiently.

Data Science and Machine Learning: Python is widely used in data science and machine learning applications, thanks to the availability of powerful libraries like NumPy, Pandas, and Scikit-learn. These libraries provide a lot of tools for data manipulation, analysis, and modeling, making Python a popular choice for data scientists and machine learning engineers.

Scientific Computing: Python is also widely used in scientific computing applications, thanks to libraries like SciPy, Matplotlib, and IPython. These libraries provide a lot of useful tools for scientific computing, such as numerical integration, optimization, and plotting.

Automation and Scripting: Python is a great language for automation and scripting tasks, thanks to its simplicity and ease of use. It's often used for tasks like web scraping, file processing, and system administration.

Education: Python is widely used in education as a first programming language, thanks to its ease of use and simplicity. Many universities and schools now teach Python as part of their computer science curriculum.

Overall, Python is a versatile language that can be used in a wide variety of applications, making it a popular choice for developers across many industries and fields.

How long will it take to learn python ??

The amount of time it takes to learn Python can vary depending on several factors, such as your previous programming experience, the amount of time you can dedicate to learning, and the level of proficiency you want to achieve. However, here are some general guidelines:

Basic proficiency: If you want to learn the basics of Python and be able to write simple scripts and programs, it can take anywhere from a few weeks to a couple of months of consistent practice.

Intermediate proficiency: If you want to be able to write more complex programs and use Python for data analysis or web development, it can take several months to a year of consistent practice.

Advanced proficiency: If you want to become an expert in Python and be able to contribute to open source projects or develop your own libraries and frameworks, it can take several years of consistent practice and study.

Keep in mind that learning Python is an ongoing process, and even experienced programmers continue to learn new things about the language and its ecosystem. The most important thing is to start practicing and learning consistently, and to focus on building projects and solving problems to reinforce your knowledge.

Basics of python

Here are some of the basics of Python programming language:

Syntax: Python has a very clean and simple syntax, with easy-to-read code that is free of semicolons and braces. Python uses indentation to define blocks of code.

Variables: In Python, variables are used to store values. Unlike other programming languages, variables do not need to be declared with a specific data type.

Data Types: Python has several built-in data types, including strings, integers, floating-point numbers, and booleans. It also supports more complex data types such as lists, dictionaries, and tuples.

Control Structures: Python has several control structures, including if/else statements, loops (for and while), and try/except blocks for handling errors.

Functions: Functions are used in Python to organize and reuse code. They are defined using the "def" keyword and can take arguments and return values.

Libraries and Modules: Python has a large standard library, as well as a vast ecosystem of third-party libraries and modules that can be imported into your code to provide additional functionality.

Object-Oriented Programming: Python is an object-oriented programming language, which means it supports the creation and use of objects and classes.

These are just some of the basics of Python programming. Learning the basics is essential to building a strong foundation for more advanced programming concepts in Python.

prerequisites to use Python on Windows machine

To run Python on a Windows machine, you will need to follow these prerequisites:

Install Python: The first step is to download and install the Python interpreter from the official Python website. You can choose either Python 2.x or Python 3.x, but it is recommended to use Python 3.x as it is the latest version.

Set the environment variables: Once you have installed Python, you need to set the environment variables. This will ensure that your computer knows where to find the Python interpreter. To set the environment variables, you need to follow these steps:

Right-click on "This PC" and select "Properties". Click on "Advanced System Settings" and then "Environment Variables". Under "System Variables", click "New" and enter "PYTHON_HOME" as the variable name and the location of the Python installation as the variable value (e.g., C:\Python39). Edit the "Path" variable and add "%PYTHON_HOME%\Scripts" and "%PYTHON_HOME%" to the list of paths.

Install a Code Editor: To write and run Python code, you will need a code editor. Some popular options for Python include PyCharm, VS Code, and Spyder.
item.author: yogini16

item.title: 为什么可访问性对您的网站至关重要：确保所有人都能公平访问 | Why Accessibility is Critical for Your Website: Ensuring Equitable Access for All
item.description:
网站可访问性是为了确保所有用户，无论是否残疾或受损，都能平等地访问您网站的内容和功能。 全球有超过 10 亿残障人士，确保每个人都可以访问您的网站比以往任何时候都更加重要。

在这篇博文中，我们将探讨为什么可访问性对您的网站至关重要，并讨论实施可访问性功能的好处。 我们还将介绍法律注意事项并提供一些实用技巧来帮助您入门。

无障碍的好处

使您的网站易于访问有很多好处。 通过提供包容性的用户体验，您正在使您的网站更加实用和用户友好。 这可以提高参与度、提高转化率和增强品牌声誉。

此外，使您的网站易于访问可以开拓新市场并吸引新的受众。 通过确保您的内容可供所有人使用，您可以吸引以前可能被排除在外的用户。

法律考虑

网站可访问性不仅是用户体验问题，也是法律要求。 包括美国在内的许多国家/地区都有法律规定某些类型的网站必须具有网站可访问性。

在美国，《美国残疾人法案》(ADA) 要求企业和组织让残疾人可以访问他们的网站。 不遵守这些规定可能导致诉讼和处罚。

入门

如果您已准备好开始让您的网站易于访问，您可以先做几件事。 首先对您的网站进行可访问性审核，这可以确定您的网站可能存在不足的地方。

从那里，您可以实现辅助功能，例如图像的替代文本、清晰简单的语言以及正确的标题结构。 您还可以考虑使用屏幕阅读器等辅助技术来测试您网站对残障用户的可用性。

要了解如何提高网站的可访问性，请参阅我的博客，详细解释每个标签和步骤。 如需更多灵感，您可以查看这篇文章。

结论

总之，网站可访问性对于确保所有用户公平访问至关重要。 通过使您的站点易于访问，您可以改善用户体验、吸引新的受众并遵守法律要求。 只需付出一点努力，您就可以对残障人士的生活产生重大影响，并创建一个更具包容性的在线社区。

感谢您阅读到这里

像这样订阅我的时事通讯以获得令人兴奋的帖子。

热情的作家分享见解和经验以启发和告知。 和我一起踏上发现和成长的旅程。

最初发表于https://blog.sarveshpatil.com 。



原文:

Website accessibility is about ensuring that all users, regardless of disability or impairment, have equal access to the content and functionality of your website. With over one billion people globally living with a disability, it is more important than ever to make sure your website is accessible to everyone.

In this blog post, we will explore why accessibility is critical for your website and discuss the benefits of implementing accessibility features. We’ll also cover legal considerations and offer some practical tips to help you get started.

The Benefits of Accessibility

There are numerous benefits to making your website accessible. By providing an inclusive user experience, you are making your website more usable and user-friendly. This can lead to increased engagement, improved conversion rates, and a stronger brand reputation.

Additionally, making your website accessible can open up new markets and reach new audiences. By ensuring that your content is available to everyone, you can attract users who may have been excluded previously.

Legal Considerations

Website accessibility is not only a matter of user experience but also a legal requirement. Many countries, including the United States, have laws that mandate website accessibility for certain types of websites.

In the US, the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) requires that businesses and organizations make their websites accessible to people with disabilities. Failure to comply with these regulations can result in lawsuits and penalties.

Getting Started

If you’re ready to start making your website accessible, there are a few things you can do to get started. Begin by conducting an accessibility audit of your website, which can identify areas where your site may be falling short.

From there, you can implement accessibility features such as alternative text for images, clear and simple language, and proper heading structure. You can also consider using assistive technology like screen readers to test the usability of your site for users with disabilities.

To Learn how to increase accessibility of your website, here’s my blog explaining every tag and steps in detail. For more Inspiration, you can check out this article.

How to Implement ARIA Tags for Better Accessibility: A Comprehensive G

Conclusion

In conclusion, website accessibility is critical for ensuring equitable access to all users. By making your site accessible, you can improve the user experience, reach new audiences, and comply with legal requirements. With a little effort, you can make a big impact on the lives of people with disabilities and create a more inclusive online community.

Thank you for reading this far

Subscribe to my Newsletter for exciting posts just like this.

Passionate writer sharing insights and experiences to inspire and inform. Join me on a journey of discovery and growth.

Originally published at https://blog.sarveshpatil.com.

item.title: Recoil 是 React 状态管理的武士刀 | Recoil is the Samurai Sword of React State Management
item.description:
今天，我采用了一种新的方式来呈现这篇文章，即添加武士风格。 我真诚地希望它能为您的阅读体验增添价值。 欢迎并感谢您的反馈，因此请在下面的评论部分分享您的想法。

在今天的冒险中，我们将看到Recoil 是什么，它是如何工作的，为什么你可能想在你的 React 应用程序中使用它等等。

带着武士的精神，让我们开始我们的冒险吧……

安装反冲 - 武士之道

您可以通过在终端中运行以下命令，使用npm或yarn安装 Recoil：

使用反冲状态的组件需要RecoilRoot出现在父树中的某个位置。 放置它的好地方是在你的根组件中：

 import React from "react"; import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client"; import { RecoilRoot } from "recoil"; import App from "./App"; ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root") as HTMLElement).render( <React.StrictMode> <RecoilRoot> <App /> </RecoilRoot> </React.StrictMode> );

这确保状态对所有需要它的子组件可用，并避免组件由于缺少RecoilRoot组件而无法访问状态的任何潜在问题。 武士之道，不容有任何差错。 所以继续保持警惕和荣誉。

什么是后坐力？

不管怎样，Recoil 提供了一种比 Context API 更强大、更灵活的方式来管理应用程序的状态。

一个原子是一个可以被组件读取和更新的状态。 组件可以订阅原子，以便在它们的值发生变化时得到通知。

选择器可用于计算来自应用程序状态的派生数据。

后坐力如何工作？

它通过定义代表应用程序状态的原子和选择器的集合来工作。 原子是使用atom()函数定义的，它以初始值作为参数。 选择器是使用selector()函数定义的，该函数采用计算派生状态的函数作为参数。 原子和选择器共同构成了 Recoil 的支柱。

创建一个原子

在武士之道中，Recoil 中的原子就像一把剑。 它是一个状态单元，可以由应用程序的不同部分读取和写入。 要创建一个原子，您可以使用 Recoil 库中的 atom 函数。

告诉你我的意思..

 import { atom } from 'recoil'; export const weaponState = atom({ key: 'weaponState', default: { name: 'Katana', damage: 50, }, });

在组件中使用原子

组件可以订阅原子和选择器。
要在组件中使用weaponState原子，您可以使用 Recoil 库中的useRecoilState钩子。

告诉你我的意思..

 import React from 'react'; import { useRecoilState } from 'recoil'; import { weaponState } from './atoms'; function WeaponDisplay() { const [weapon, setWeapon] = useRecoilState(weaponState); return ( <div> <h1>{weapon.name}</h1> <p>Damage: {weapon.damage}</p> <button onClick={() => setWeapon({ name: 'Nodachi', damage: 70 })}> Upgrade Weapon </button> </div> ); }

按照上面的例子，我们使用useRecoilState钩子来访问weaponState原子及其当前值。 我们还提供了一个按钮，允许用户通过将原子设置为新值来升级他们的武器。

创建选择器

选择器是允许您从Recoil 原子派生状态的函数。 它们可用于计算复杂值或聚合来自多个原子的数据。

告诉你我的意思..

 import { selector } from 'recoil'; import { weaponState } from './atoms'; export const weaponDamageSelector = selector({ key: 'weaponDamageSelector', get: ({ get }) => { const weapon = get(weaponState); return weapon.damage; }, });

在组件中使用选择器

然后我们可以使用与atoms相同的useRecoilValue钩子在我们的组件中使用选择器。

告诉你我的意思..

 import React from 'react'; import { useRecoilState } from 'recoil'; import { weaponDamageSelector } from './selectors'; function WeaponDisplay() { const weaponDamage = useRecoilState(weaponDamageSelector); return ( <div> <p>Weapon damage: {weaponDamage}</p> </div> ); }

创建一个原子族

在武士世界中，一个氏族可能有多个武士，每个武士都有自己独特的特征和能力。 同样，在 React 应用程序中，您可能有多个组件需要共享状态但具有不同的标识符或键。

这就是atomFamily派上用场的地方。 它允许您创建一个具有相同结构但具有不同键或标识符的原子族。 举个例子，假设我们想要创建一个原子家族，代表一个氏族中不同武士的武器。

 import { atomFamily } from 'recoil'; export const weaponAtomFamily = atomFamily({ key: 'weaponAtomFamily', default: id => ({ name: `Weapon - ${id}`, damage: 50, }), });

在上面的例子中，默认函数中的id参数是族中每个原子的唯一标识符。 因此，当我们调用weaponAtomFamily('Wakizashiv')时，将使用 'Wakizashiv' 作为id参数调用默认函数，并将返回表示该武士默认武器的对象。

在组件中使用 atomFamily

但是默认值也可以参数化。 这意味着您可以提供一个接受参数值并返回实际默认值的函数。

 import React from 'react'; import { useRecoilState } from 'recoil'; import { weaponAtomFamily } from './atoms'; function WeaponDisplay() { const [weapon, setWeapon] = useRecoilState(weaponAtomFamily('Wakizashiv')); return ( <div> <h1>{weapon.name}</h1> <p>Damage: {weapon.damage}</p> <button onClick={() => setWeapon({ name: 'Weapon - Nodachi', damage: 70 })}> Upgrade Weapon </button> </div> ); }

为什么要使用 Recoil？

让我们看看您可能想在 React 项目中使用 Recoil 的一些原因：

简化状态管理

使用Recoil，您可以定义表示应用程序状态的原子和选择器。 组件可以订阅这些原子和选择器，以便在它们的值发生变化时得到通知。

没有样板

它允许您使用最少的样板代码定义原子和选择器。
另一方面，在Redux中，您需要定义动作、动作创建者、缩减器和存储对象来管理应用程序的状态。

更好的性能

这是因为 Recoil 使用了一种称为依赖图的新算法，可以更有效地跟踪原子和选择器之间的依赖关系。 熟悉的语法

对于已经熟悉 React 的开发人员来说，这使得 Recoil 易于学习和使用。

灵活且可扩展

它可用于管理具有复杂状态要求的小型或大型应用程序的状态。 它允许您定义表示应用程序状态不同部分的原子和选择器，并提供用于管理这些原子和选择器之间依赖关系的工具。

所以我的武士们，让我们作为无所畏惧的武士勇士一起进入我们冒险的最后一章......

什么是原子效应？

副作用是任何影响原子状态之外的操作。 例如网络请求、本地存储或日志记录。

原子效果通过效果选项附加到原子。 每个原子都可以引用这些原子效果函数的数组，这些函数在原子初始化时按优先顺序调用。

在 Atom Effects 的帮助下，您可以向原子添加订阅，只要原子的值发生变化，订阅就会被触发。 订阅可以执行您的应用程序所需的任何副作用。 副作用可以是同步的或异步的。

例如，您可能希望在用户更改设置页面时将他们的首选项保存到本地存储。

原子效应是塑造我们周围世界的隐藏力量，就像武士的剑是由其原子的不可见相互作用塑造的一样。

告诉你我的意思..

 import { atom, useRecoilState } from 'recoil'; export const weaponState = atom({ key: "weaponState", default: { name: "Katana", damage: 50, }, /* Atom Effects */ effects: [ ({ onSet }) => { onSet(weapon => { console.log(`The weapon has upgrade to ${weapon.name}`); }); }, ], }); function WeaponDisplay() { const [weapon, setWeapon] = useRecoilState(weaponState); return ( <div> <h1>{weapon.name}</h1> <button onClick={() => setWeapon({ name: 'Nodachi' })}> Upgrade Weapon </button> </div> ); }

只要weaponState原子的值发生变化，就会调用onSet回调。 在上面的例子中，我们只是将原子的新武器记录到控制台，但我们也可以轻松地执行更复杂的副作用。

冒险结束

它允许您定义表示应用程序状态的原子和选择器。 组件可以订阅这些原子和选择器，以便在它们的值发生变化时得到通知。 如果你正在为你的 React 项目寻找一个状态管理库，Recoil 绝对值得考虑。

武士们，让我们学习后坐力的艺术，掌握状态管理的方法。

动机

请考虑通过订阅我们的频道来关注和支持我们。 非常感谢您的支持，这将帮助我们继续创作供您欣赏的内容。 预先感谢您对我们的支持！

推特



原文:

Today, I have taken a new approach to present this article by adding a Samurai touch. I sincerely hope that it adds value to your reading experience. Your feedback is always welcome and appreciated so please share your thoughts in the comments section below..

Recoil was created to provide an alternative to Redux which is another popular state management library for React. In today's adventure, we will see what Recoil is, how it works, why you might want to use it in your React apps and much more..

With a spirit of the samurai, let's begin our adventure..

Installing Recoil - The Way of the Samurai

You can install Recoil using npm or yarn by running the following command in your terminal:

Components that use recoil state need RecoilRoot to appear somewhere in the parent tree. A good place to put this is in your root component:

import React from "react";
import ReactDOM from "react-dom/client";
import { RecoilRoot } from "recoil";
import App from "./App"; ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root") as HTMLElement).render( <React.StrictMode> <RecoilRoot> <App /> </RecoilRoot> </React.StrictMode>
);

This ensures that the state is available to all child components that require it and avoids any potential issues with components not being able to access the state due to missing RecoilRoot components. In the way of the samurai, there is no room for error. So proceed with vigilance and honor.

What is Recoil?

Recoil is a state management library for React that was released by Facebook in 2020. It provides a simple and intuitive way to manage the state of a React application. Recoil is built on top of React's Context API which is used to pass data down the component tree. Anyway, Recoil provides a more powerful and flexible way to manage the state of an application than the Context API.

The core idea behind Recoil is that state should be defined as a collection of atoms. An atom is a piece of state that can be read and updated by components. Components can subscribe to atoms to be notified when their value changes.

Recoil also has a secret weapon - The Selectors, powerful functions that derive new state from atoms or other selectors. Selectors can be used to compute derived data from the state of an application.

How does Recoil work?

The art of Recoil is rooted in the samurai's keen understanding of the world around them. It works by defining a collection of atoms and selectors that represent the state of an application. Atoms are defined using the atom() function, it takes an initial value as a parameter. Selectors are defined using the selector() function which takes a function that computes the derived state as a parameter. Together, atoms and selectors form the backbone of Recoil.

Create an atom

In the way of the Samurai, an atom in Recoil is like a sword. It's an unit of state that can be read and written to by different parts of your app. To create an atom you can use the atom function from the Recoil library.

To show you what I mean..

import { atom } from 'recoil'; export const weaponState = atom({ key: 'weaponState', default: { name: 'Katana', damage: 50, },
});

Use the atom in a component

Components can subscribe to atoms and selectors.
To use the weaponState atom in a component you can use the useRecoilState hook from the Recoil library.

To show you what I mean..

import React from 'react';
import { useRecoilState } from 'recoil';
import { weaponState } from './atoms'; function WeaponDisplay() { const [weapon, setWeapon] = useRecoilState(weaponState); return ( <div> <h1>{weapon.name}</h1> <p>Damage: {weapon.damage}</p> <button onClick={() => setWeapon({ name: 'Nodachi', damage: 70 })}> Upgrade Weapon </button> </div> );
}

As per above instance, we're using the useRecoilState hook to access the weaponState atom and its current value. We're also providing a button that allows the user to upgrade their weapon by setting the atom to a new value.

Create a selector

Selectors are functions that allow you to derive state from your Recoil atoms. They can be useful for computing complex values or aggregating data from multiple atoms.

To show you what I mean..

import { selector } from 'recoil';
import { weaponState } from './atoms'; export const weaponDamageSelector = selector({ key: 'weaponDamageSelector', get: ({ get }) => { const weapon = get(weaponState); return weapon.damage; },
});

Use selector in a component

We can then use selector in our components using the useRecoilValue hook same as atoms.

To show you what I mean..

import React from 'react';
import { useRecoilState } from 'recoil';
import { weaponDamageSelector } from './selectors'; function WeaponDisplay() { const weaponDamage = useRecoilState(weaponDamageSelector); return ( <div> <p>Weapon damage: {weaponDamage}</p> </div> );
}

Create an atomFamily

In the samurai world, a clan may have multiple samurais and each with their own unique characteristics and abilities. Similarly, in a React application, you may have multiple components that need to share state but with different identifiers or keys.

This is where atomFamily comes in handy. It allows you to create a family of atoms that share the same structure, but with different keys or identifiers. As an instance, let's say we want to create a family of atoms that represent the weapons of different samurais in a clan.

import { atomFamily } from 'recoil'; export const weaponAtomFamily = atomFamily({ key: 'weaponAtomFamily', default: id => ({ name: `Weapon - ${id}`, damage: 50, }),
});

In above instance, id argument in the default function is the unique identifier for each atom in the family. So when we call weaponAtomFamily('Wakizashiv'), the default function will be called with 'Wakizashiv' as the id argument and will return an object representing the default weapon for that samurai.

Use atomFamily in a component

An atomFamily() takes almost the same options as a simple atom(). But the default value can also be parameterized. That means you could provide a function which takes the parameter value and returns the actual default value.

import React from 'react';
import { useRecoilState } from 'recoil';
import { weaponAtomFamily } from './atoms'; function WeaponDisplay() { const [weapon, setWeapon] = useRecoilState(weaponAtomFamily('Wakizashiv')); return ( <div> <h1>{weapon.name}</h1> <p>Damage: {weapon.damage}</p> <button onClick={() => setWeapon({ name: 'Weapon - Nodachi', damage: 70 })}> Upgrade Weapon </button> </div> );
}

Why use Recoil?

Recoil provides several benefits over other state management libraries for React such as Redux. Let's see some reasons why you might want to use Recoil in your React projects:

Simplified state management

Recoil provides a simpler and more intuitive way to manage the state of a React app. With Recoil you can define atoms and selectors that represent the state of your app. Components can subscribe to these atoms and selectors to be notified when their value changes.

No boilerplate

Recoil also reduces the amount of boilerplate code that is required to manage the state of a React app. It allows you to define atoms and selectors with minimal boilerplate code.
On otherside, in Redux you need to define actions, action creators, reducers and store objects to manage the state of your app.

Better performance

Recoil provides better performance than other state management libraries for React. This is because Recoil uses a new algorithm called the Dependency Graph which allows it to track the dependencies between atoms and selectors more efficiently. The Dependency Graph ensures that only the components that need to be re-rendered are re-rendered when the state of an atom or selector changes which improves the performance of your app.

Familiar syntax

Recoil uses a syntax that is similar to React's built in hooks such as useState() and useEffect(). This makes Recoil easy to learn and use for developers who are already familiar with React.

Flexible and scalable

Recoil is designed to be flexible and scalable. It can be used to manage the state of small or large apps with complex state requirements. It allows you to define atoms and selectors that represent different parts of your app's state and it provides tools for managing the dependencies between these atoms and selectors.

Battle of Recoil and Redux

So my fellow samurais, let's move forward to the final chapter of our adventure together as fearless samurai warriors..

What is Atom Effects?

Atom Effects is a feature in Recoil that allows you to execute side effects when an atom's value changes. Side effects are any operations that have an impact outside of the atom's state. Such as network requests, local storage or logging.

Atom effects are attached to atoms via the effects option. Each atom can reference an array of these atom effect functions which are called in priority order when the atom is initialized.

With the help of Atom Effects, you can add subscriptions to an atom that will be triggered whenever the atom's value changes. The subscriptions can execute any side effects that are necessary for your app. The side effects can be synchronous or asynchronous.

Atom Effects can be useful in situations where you need to execute side effects based on the current state of your app. As instance, you may want to save the user's preferences to local storage whenever they make a change to a settings page.

Atom Effects are the hidden forces that shape the world around us, just as a samurai's sword is shaped by the unseen interactions of its atoms.

To show you what I mean..

import { atom, useRecoilState } from 'recoil'; export const weaponState = atom({ key: "weaponState", default: { name: "Katana", damage: 50, }, /* Atom Effects */ effects: [ ({ onSet }) => { onSet(weapon => { console.log(`The weapon has upgrade to ${weapon.name}`); }); }, ],
}); function WeaponDisplay() { const [weapon, setWeapon] = useRecoilState(weaponState); return ( <div> <h1>{weapon.name}</h1> <button onClick={() => setWeapon({ name: 'Nodachi' })}> Upgrade Weapon </button> </div> );
}

The onSet callback is called whenever the value of the weaponState atom changes. In above instance, we're simply logging the new weapon of the atom to the console, but we could just as easily execute a more complex side effect.

End of adventure

Recoil is built on top of React's Context API and provides a more powerful and flexible way to manage the state of an application than the Context API. It allows you to define atoms and selectors that represent the state of your app. Components can subscribe to these atoms and selectors to be notified when their value changes. Recoil provides several benefits over other state management libraries for React such as simplified state management, no boilerplate, better performance, familiar syntax and flexibility and scalability. If you're looking for a state management library for your React projects, Recoil is definitely worth considering.

My fellow samurais, let's take up the art of Recoil and master the ways of state management.

Motivation

🍀Support

Please consider following and supporting us by subscribing to our channel. Your support is greatly appreciated and will help us continue creating content for you to enjoy. Thank you in advance for your support!

YouTube
GitHub
Twitter
item.author: codeofrelevancy

item.title: 在 JavaScript 中使用 Map、Filter 和 Reduce 掌握数组操作 | Mastering Array Manipulation with Map, Filter, and Reduce in JavaScript
item.description:
安纳卡敦

发布于 2 月 27 日

了解这些方法并知道在何处应用它们很重要。 在本文中，您还将学习如何创建自己的自定义方法。

开始吧…

地图
根据 mdn： map() 方法创建一个新数组，其中填充了对调用数组中的每个元素调用提供的函数的结果。

当您必须对数组中的每个值运行一个操作时，通常会使用它。

这是一般语法：
const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] let transformedArray = arr.map((item) => { return item * 2 })

下面是自定义 map() 方法的示例：

const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] Array.prototype.myMap = function (cb) { let newArr = []; for (let arr of this) { newArr.push(cb(arr)) } return newArr; } let transformedArray = arr.myMap((item) => { return item * 2 })

简单来说，filter就是用来过滤掉数组的。 它返回包含成功通过所提供条件的值的数组。

这是一般语法：
const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] let transformedArray = arr.filter((item) => { return item > 2 })

以下是自定义 filter() 方法的示例：

const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; Array.prototype.myFilter = function (cb) { const filtered = []; for (let el of this) { if (cb(el)) { filtered.push(el); } } return filtered; }; const filteredData = arr.myFilter((item) => { return item > 2 });

在数组的所有元素上运行 reducer 的最终结果是一个单一的值。 简而言之，请记住当您必须遍历数组并找到特定值时使用 reduce。 它有两个参数：第一个是回调函数(cb)，第二个是传递给累加器的initialValue。

减少（callbackFn，初始值）

回调函数有两个参数，累加器(acc) 和当前值(curr)。
reduce(function (acc, curr) { /* … */ })

累加器是先前调用回调函数 (cb) 的结果。 在第一次调用时，如果未提供 initialValue，则默认值为 array[0]。 如果指定了 initialValue，cb 将以数组中的第一个值作为 curr 开始执行。 如果未指定 initialValue，则将 acc 初始化为数组中的第一个值，cb 以数组中的第二个值作为 curr 开始执行。 在这种情况下，如果数组为空（因此没有第一个值作为累加器返回），则会引发错误。

这是一般语法：

const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; const sum = arr.reduce((acc, curr) => { acc = acc + curr; return acc },0)

如果我们将记录 acc 和 curr

const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; const sum = arr.reduce((acc, curr) => { console.log('acc--> ' + acc, 'curr--> ' + curr) acc = acc + curr; return acc }, 0) //log output acc--> 0 curr--> 1 acc--> 1 curr--> 2 acc--> 3 curr--> 3 acc--> 6 curr--> 4

下面是自定义 reduce() 方法的示例：

const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; Array.prototype.myReduce = function (cb, value) { let a = this; for (let el of this) { value = value !== undefined ? cb(value, el) : el; } return value; }; const sum = arr.myReduce(function (acc, curr) { return acc + curr; }, 0);

控制台日志（总和）； 结论总之，map、filter 和 reduce 是强大的方法，可用于转换、过滤和计算数组中的值。 通过了解这些方法并知道在哪里应用它们，您可以大大提高代码的效率和可读性。 此外，基于这些方法创建您自己的自定义函数可以为您的编程提供更大的灵活性。 无论您是初学者还是经验丰富的开发人员，掌握 map、filter 和 reduce 都是编写高质量代码必不可少的。 所以，一定要练习和试验这些方法，以成为一个更熟练的程序员。

谢谢你，祝你编码愉快✌️

一旦暂停，amnahk 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，amnahk 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，amnahk 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 amnahk 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 amnahk：

降低 amnahk 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 amnahk 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Amnah Khatun

Posted on Feb 27

The methods of map, filter, and reduce are widely used by developers and are commonly asked in interviews. Understanding these methods and knowing where to apply them is important. In this article, you will also learn how to create your own custom methods.

Let's start…

MAP
As per mdn: The map() method creates a new array populated with the results of calling a provided function on every element in the calling array.

map() is used to transform the array's values and return the new array. It is generally used when you have to run an operation for each value in the array. 

Here's the general syntax:
const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] let transformedArray = arr.map((item) => { return item * 2 })

console.log(transformedArray) //[ 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 ]

Here's an example of a custom map() method:

const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] Array.prototype.myMap = function (cb) { let newArr = []; for (let arr of this) { newArr.push(cb(arr)) } return newArr; } let transformedArray = arr.myMap((item) => { return item * 2 })

console.log(transformedArray) //[ 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 ]

FILTER As per mdn, filter() method creates a shallow copy of a portion of a given array, filtered down to just the elements from the given array that pass the test implemented by the provided function.

In simple words, filter is used to filter out the array. It returns the array with values that successfully passed the provided condition.

Here's the general syntax:
const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] let transformedArray = arr.filter((item) => { return item > 2 })

console.log(transformedArray)// [ 3, 4, 5 ]

Here's an example of a custom filter() method:

const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; Array.prototype.myFilter = function (cb) { const filtered = []; for (let el of this) { if (cb(el)) { filtered.push(el); } } return filtered; }; const filteredData = arr.myFilter((item) => { return item > 2 });

console.log(filteredData) //[3,4,5]

REDUCE As per the mdn, reduce() method executes a user-supplied "reducer" callback function on each element of the array, in order, to pass in the return value from the calculation on the preceding element. The final result of running the reducer across all elements of the array is a single value. To break it down in simple words, remember that reduce is used when you have to iterate over the array and find a particular value. It takes two parameters: the first is the callback function(cb), and the second is the initialValue, which is passed to the accumulator.

reduce(callbackFn, initialValue)

The callback function takes two parameters, accumulator(acc) and current value(curr). 
reduce(function (acc, curr) { /* … */ })

The accumulator is the result from the previous call of callback function(cb). On the first call, in the case initialValue is not provided, the default value is array[0]. curris the value of the current element.
If initialValue is specified, cb starts executing with the first value in the array as curr. If initialValue is not specified, acc is initialized to the first value in the array, and cb starts executing with the second value in the array as curr. In this case, if the array is empty (so that there's no first value to return as accumulator), an error is thrown.

Here's the general syntax:

const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; const sum = arr.reduce((acc, curr) => { acc = acc + curr; return acc },0)

console.log(sum) //15

If we will log acc and curr

const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; const sum = arr.reduce((acc, curr) => { console.log('acc--> ' + acc, 'curr--> ' + curr) acc = acc + curr; return acc }, 0) //log output acc--> 0 curr--> 1 acc--> 1 curr--> 2 acc--> 3 curr--> 3 acc--> 6 curr--> 4

acc--> 10 curr--> 5

Here's an example of a custom reduce() method:

const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; Array.prototype.myReduce = function (cb, value) { let a = this; for (let el of this) { value = value !== undefined ? cb(value, el) : el; } return value; }; const sum = arr.myReduce(function (acc, curr) { return acc + curr; }, 0);

console.log(sum); //15

Conclusion In conclusion, map, filter, and reduce are powerful methods that can be used to transform, filter, and calculate values in arrays. By understanding these methods and knowing where to apply them, you can greatly improve the efficiency and readability of your code. In addition, creating your own custom functions based on these methods can provide even more flexibility in your programming. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced developer, mastering map, filter, and reduce is essential to writing high-quality code. So, make sure to practice and experiment with these methods to become a more proficient programmer.

Thank you and happy coding ✌️

☝️ Check out this all-time classic DEV post

Odukwe Precious Chiemeka - Feb 14

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging amnahk will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 每次测试运行后为什么以及如何重置数据库（Cypress、Mongo 5.0.11、React 示例） | The why and how to reset the database after each test run (Cypress, Mongo 5.0.11, React example)
item.description:
作为一名开发人员，我经常发现自己从事的项目缺乏专门的测试团队或资源。 为了最大限度地减少部署到暂存之前我必须执行的手动回归测试的数量，我发现为最关键的场景编写端到端 (e2e) 测试是最有效的解决方案。 我首选的 e2e 测试工具是 Cypress。

然而，经常出现的一个问题是：我们如何确保这些测试中的每一个都可以通过？

  • 多次运行；
  • 独立运行。

这就是为什么我们需要一种在每次测试后重置数据库的方法。

我的方法基于Gleb Bahmutov 的帖子。 不同的是，这篇文章是针对 mongo 5.0.11 的，而他的文章是针对较旧的 mongo 版本的。 如果您使用mongosh在 docker 终端中使用您的数据库，那么我的帖子很可能适合您！

与 Gleb 的帖子的另一个不同之处在于，我将讨论的所有测试都与应用程序进入流程有关：注册、登录和登录测试。

让我们考虑以下示例（存储库在这里）

顺便说一句，赛普拉斯 建议在每次测试前清理数据库。 我在帖子标题中写了“after”只是为了 SEO 目的。

注册测试

您已经编写了一个注册场景测试。 第一次测试通过。 您尝试再次运行测试并收到错误“电子邮件地址已存在”：

您可能想跳过我在下面写的内容，而只是查看存储库中的分支feature-cypress-reset 。 在那里您会看到一个可以无限次运行的注册测试。

文件 cypress/support/db.js 有助于连接到数据库。

如果你是前端，你不必太担心重构这个文件。 将您的 PR 转换为工作版本的 Devops 会将uri和client.db("rests")更改为适合您公司的内容。 他们将建立一个单独的测试环境，其中包括一个测试数据库。 该测试环境将允许运行测试，而不必担心损坏或损坏生产系统中的重要数据。

最有趣的文件是cypress.config.ts 。 我花了一些时间试图理解为什么 await users.remove({})在我意识到它在较新的 mongo 中被弃用并被deleteMany取代之前不起作用！

然后你只需添加一个用于清理的 cypress 命令：

并像这样在测试中使用它：

登录测试

当我开始为登录用户编写测试时，我发现 Cypress 不喜欢运行时间过长的测试。 如果测试花费太多时间，Cypress 可能会抛出以下错误之一（有关 GitHub 的问题 -第一，第二）：

  • 中止测试运行并显示一些关于网络套接字的消息；
  • 中止在表单字段中键入内容，例如，不要键入“ somebody@gmail.com ”，而是键入“somebody@gma”。

对于此类测试，您可以做的最好的事情就是使用快捷方式。 例如，如果通过在表单中​​键入值来模拟用户登录，则使用 Cypress 命令发送 POST 请求。 或者直接使用 Cypress 任务更改数据库记录。
此外，此设置可能会有所帮助：

赛普拉斯建议在测试中进行会话切换，但我们不使用它。 可悲的是，我不记得关于为什么它对我们当前的项目没有用的任何细节。

假设您想测试登录流程。 在测试之前，您的数据库中需要有一个用户！

您可能想知道我如何获得我决定使用的密码的加密版本。 我临时在app.js中放入了如下代码：

登录测试

假设我们想为登录用户测试一些东西。 我们将使用快捷方式登录，正如 Cypress 文档中所建议的那样：

然后我们将像这样使用它：

结论

我想介绍更多关于 Cypress 的信息，但这篇文章已经太长了 :( 我唯一想（再次）提及的是，如果您是一名担心 QA 但没有太多时间关注 Cypress 的开发人员根据您花费多少时间编写/维护测试与测试多少功能的比率，绝对是最佳选择。



原文:

As a developer, I frequently find myself working on projects that lack a dedicated testing team or resources. To minimize the amount of manual regression testing I have to do before deployment to staging, I've found that writing end-to-end (e2e) tests for the most critical scenarios is the most effective solution. My preferred tool for e2e testing is Cypress.

However, a question that often arises is: how can we ensure that each of these tests can be

  • run multiple times;
  • run independently.

That's why we need a way to reset the database after each test.

My approach is based on Gleb Bahmutov's post. The difference is that this post is for mongo 5.0.11, and his post is for older mongo versions. If you use mongosh to play with your database in docker terminal, then most probably my post is for you!

Another difference from Gleb's post is that all the tests I'll talk about will be about app entry flows: Sign up, Log in and Logged in tests.

Let's consider a following example (repository is here)

Btw Cypress recommends to clean up database before each test. I wrote "after" in the post header only for SEO purposes.

Sign up Test

You have written a test for sign up scenario. First time test passed. You try to run the test again and get an error "Email address already exists":

You might want to skip what I've written below and just take a look to branch feature-cypress-reset in the repository instead. There you will see a signup test that can be run an indefinite amount of times.

File cypress/support/db.js helps to connect to the database.

If you are a frontend, you don't have to worry much about refactoring this file. Devops that will convert your PR into working version will change uri and client.db("rests") to something that works for your company. They will set up a separate testing environment that includes a test database. This test environment will allow run tests without the fear of damaging or corrupting important data in the production system.

The most interesting file to look at is cypress.config.ts. I spent some time trying to understand why await users.remove({}) doesn't work before I realised that it was deprecated in newer mongo and replaced with deleteMany!

Then you just add a cypress command for cleanup:

And use it in the test like this:

Login Test

When I've started to write tests for logged in users, I've discovered that Cypress doesn't like tests that take too much time to run. If test takes too much time, Cypress can throw one of the following errors (there are GitHub issues about that - first, second):

  • abort test running with some message about web sockets;
  • abort typing into form fields, e.g. instead of typing "somebody@gmail.com" type "somebody@gma".

The best thing is you can do for such tests is to use shortcuts. E.g. instead if imitating user login by typing values into forms, send a POST request using Cypress command. Or change a database record directly using Cypress task.
Also, this setting might help:

Cypress suggests to do session switching inside tests but we don't use it. Sadly I don't remember any details about why it wasn't useful for our current project.

Let's say you want to test login flow. You'll need to have a user in your DB before the test!

You might wonder how I got an encrypted version for password that I decided to use. I temporarily put in app.js the following code:

Logged in Test

Let's say we want to test something for a logged in user. We will use a shortcut for logging in, as it is advised in Cypress docs:

Then we'll use it like this:

Conclusion

I would like to tell more about Cypress but this post is too long already :( The only thing that I would like to mention (again) is that if you are a dev who worries about QA but doesn't have much time for it Cypress is definitely the best choice based on the ratio how much time you spend writing/maintaining tests vs how much functionality you get tested.

    • 使用values()方法：我们可以使用 values() 方法返回字典中所有值的列表。
    • 使用items()方法：我们可以使用 items() 方法以元组的形式返回键值对列表。

    • 一个类名可以在不同的元素中使用任意次数

    原文:

    Anirban Das

    Posted on Feb 27

       

    1. Why is it recommended to use semantic elements when we can just make an entire website comprising div elements?

    Answer:

    • They are more descriptive than divs.
    • Easy for developers to read and understand.
    • Screen readers and browsers can interpret Semantic HTML better, which makes it more accessible.
    • Also helps in page ranking for search engine(SEO)

    2. What is the difference between WHERE and HAVING processes in SQL?

    Answer:

    • The WHERE clause is used to filter rows based on a specified condition, while the HAVING clause is used to filter groups based on a specified condition.
    • The HAVING clause can only be used in conjunction with the GROUP BY clause.

    3. What are void elements in HTML?

    Answer:

    • HTML elements whose content model never allows it to have contents under any circumstance.
    • They do not have closing tags or do not need to be closed.
    • They cannot have any Child Nodes(i.e., nested elements or text nodes).
    • For Example <br />, <img />, <hr />, etc.

    4. Why do we use dunder methods in python?

    Answer:
    A dunder method is a method in Python that has a double underscore prefix and suffix in its name, such as init or str. These methods are also known as "magic methods" or "special methods" because they define special behavior for built-in Python operations.

    For example, the _init_ method is called when an object is created and allows you to define how the object should be initialized. The _str_ method is called when an object is converted to a string and allows you to define how the object should be represented as a string.

    5. What is the difference between padding and margin in HTML?

    Answer:
    The margin of an element represents the outside space of the element itself, while the padding represents the inner space surrounding the element.

    6. What is the difference between git and GitHub?

    Answer:

    Git GitHub
    Git is a version control system to manage source code history GitHub is a hosting service for Git repositories
    Git is installed locally on the system GitHub is hosted on the web
    Git is a command-line tool GitHub is a graphical user interface
    Git has no user management feature GitHub has a built-in user management feature

    7. What are the different methods used for accessing the values of a dictionary?

    Answer:

    • Using keys to access values: We can access the value of a dictionary by specifying its key in square brackets.
    • Using the values() method: We can use the values() method to return a list of all the values in a dictionary.
    • Using the items() method: We can use the items() method to return a list of key-value pairs in the form of tuples.
    • Using a loop to iterate through keys or values: We can use a loop to iterate through the keys or values of a dictionary.
    • Using the get() method: We can use the get() method to retrieve the value associated with a key in a dictionary.

    8. How do we stage all files except a specific file without using "gitignore"?

    Answer:

    • First step is to add all the files to the staging area using
    • Then remove the specific file from the staging area using git reset

    9. Difference between class and id in HTML and CSS.

    Answer:

    Both are attributes of HTML elements and can be used as a selector in css, but the main difference lies that

    • A Class name can be used any number of times with different elements
    • Whereas ID name is specific and can be used only once within a HTML page

    10. How to revert a specific commit pushed to git due to human error?

    Answer: To revert a commit in Git, use the command:

    Replace <commit> with the commit hash which can be found from:

    command by copying previous commit hash.

    11. While applying different selectors in HTML and CSS, what is the order of Specificity?

    Answer:

    • Inline styles (Highest Specificity)
    • ID
    • Classes, pseudo-classes
    • Elements and pseudo-elements (Lowest Specificity)

    12. In what scenarios is grid better than flex and vice-versa?

    Answer:

    • We use grid when the design needs items in the grid to line up in columns and rows
    • We use flex when we want items to get stack up independently on others in terms of length and alignment

    13. What is the difference between SQL and NoSQL database?

    Answer:

    SQL NoSQL
    It is a Relation Database Management System It is a Non-Relational Database Management System
    Stores data in the form of tables with rows and columns Stores data in various format including document-oriented, key-value, graph-based models
    SQL databases have a fixed schema, which means that the data structure must be defined before data is stored NoSQL databases have a dynamic schema, which allows for more flexibility in storing and manipulating data
    They can handle limited amounts of data They can handle large amounts of data

    14. Do we have "closures" in python?

    Answer:

    Yes, Python has closures. In Python, a closure is a function object that has access to variables in its enclosing lexical scope, even when the function is called outside that scope. This allows for data to be encapsulated and protected from being modified outside the closure.

    15. What are the differences between the operators '=','==' and, 'is' keyword in python?

    Answer:

    • The '=' operator is used for variable assignment
    • The '==' operator is used for comparing values for equality. It checks only its values and not its type
    • Whereas 'is' keyword is used to compare objects/variables on its value and also on its type

    16. What are meta tags and why do we use them in HTML?

    Answer:

    • Meta tags are HTML tags that provide metadata about a web page. They are placed in the head section of an HTML document and are not visible to the user
    • Meta tags are used because they provide information that can help improve the visibility and ranking of a web page in search results.
    • They also provide information about the web page to users such as screenreaders.

    Each will generate pure CSS without the need for JS or any external libraries.

item.title: 软件开发中有效项目管理的技巧 | Tips for effective project management in software development
item.description:
有效的项目管理对于确保软件开发项目的成功至关重要。 通过正确的项目规划、执行和监控实践，团队可以更高效地工作、按时交付并交付满足客户要求的高质量软件。 在本文中，我们将探讨在软件开发中进行有效项目管理的一些最佳技巧和实践。

  1. 定义项目目标
    成功的项目管理的第一步是定义项目的目的和目标。 这涉及确定项目的要求、范围和目的。 让参与项目的每个人都在同一页面上，包括利益相关者、客户和团队成员，这一点至关重要。

  2. 制定项目计划
    明确定义的项目计划对于软件开发项目的成功至关重要。 该计划应包括项目时间表、资源分配、风险管理和沟通策略。 该计划还应包括里程碑和截止日期，团队成员可以使用这些里程碑和截止日期来跟踪进度并确保他们满足项目要求。

  3. 建立强大的团队
    软件开发项目的成功在很大程度上取决于相关团队成员的技能和专业知识。 重要的是要建立一支由对项目充满热情并愿意共同努力以取得成功的有才华和积极进取的个人组成的团队。

  4. 使用敏捷方法
    敏捷方法已成为一种流行的软件开发方法，因为它提高了灵活性、协作性和适应性。 敏捷项目通常分为冲刺，每个冲刺专注于一组特定的任务或目标。 团队协同工作以完成每个冲刺并在此过程中进行任何必要的更改或调整。

  5. 有效沟通
    有效的沟通是软件开发项目管理成功的关键。 应鼓励团队成员在整个项目过程中分享想法、提出问题并提供反馈。 定期的团队会议和状态更新有助于让每个人都保持一致，并确保项目按计划进行。

  6. 监控进度并做出调整
    监控进度并根据需要进行调整对于确保项目保持在正轨至关重要。 定期的状态报告和进度更新有助于及早发现任何潜在问题，使团队能够在项目脱轨之前采取纠正措施。

  7. 尽早并经常测试
    测试是软件开发的重要组成部分，应该从一开始就纳入项目计划。 尽早并经常进行测试有助于在错误和其他问题成为主要问题之前识别它们。 从长远来看，这可以节省时间和资源，并确保最终产品满足客户要求。

  8. 记录一切
    文档是软件开发和项目管理的重要组成部分。 保留项目要求、时间表和进度的详细记录可以帮助确保每个人都在同一页面上，并且可以在需要时回顾以前的决定和讨论。

有效的项目管理对于软件开发项目的成功至关重要。 通过遵循这些项目规划、执行和监控的技巧和最佳实践，团队可以更高效地工作、按时交付并交付满足客户要求的高质量软件。

谢谢阅读...

原文:

Effective project management is essential to ensure the success of software development projects. With the right project planning, execution, and monitoring practices, teams can work more efficiently, meet deadlines, and deliver high-quality software that meets customer requirements. In this article, we'll explore some of the best tips and practices for effective project management in software development.

  1. Define Project Goals and Objectives
    The first step to successful project management is to define the goals and objectives of the project. This involves identifying the requirements, scope, and purpose of the project. It's essential to get everyone involved in the project on the same page, including stakeholders, customers, and team members.

  2. Develop a Project Plan
    A well-defined project plan is crucial to the success of a software development project. This plan should include project timelines, resource allocation, risk management, and communication strategies. The plan should also include milestones and deadlines that team members can use to track progress and ensure they're meeting project requirements.

  3. Build a Strong Team
    The success of a software development project depends largely on the skills and expertise of the team members involved. It's important to build a team of talented and motivated individuals who are passionate about the project and are willing to work together to achieve success.

  4. Use Agile Methodology
    Agile methodology has become a popular approach to software development because it promotes flexibility, collaboration, and adaptability. Agile projects are typically divided into sprints, with each sprint focusing on a specific set of tasks or objectives. The team works collaboratively to complete each sprint and make any necessary changes or adjustments along the way.

  5. Communicate Effectively
    Effective communication is key to successful project management in software development. Team members should be encouraged to share ideas, ask questions, and provide feedback throughout the project. Regular team meetings and status updates can help keep everyone on the same page and ensure that the project is progressing as planned.

  6. Monitor Progress and Make Adjustments
    Monitoring progress and making adjustments as needed is essential to ensure that the project stays on track. Regular status reports and progress updates can help identify any potential issues early on, allowing the team to take corrective action before the project is derailed.

  7. Test Early and Often
    Testing is an important part of software development and should be incorporated into the project plan from the beginning. Testing early and often can help identify bugs and other issues before they become major problems. This can save time and resources in the long run and ensure that the final product meets customer requirements.

  8. Document Everything
    Documentation is an essential part of software development and project management. Keeping detailed records of project requirements, timelines, and progress can help ensure that everyone is on the same page and can refer back to previous decisions and discussions if needed.

Effective project management is critical to the success of software development projects. By following these tips and best practices for project planning, execution, and monitoring, teams can work more efficiently, meet deadlines, and deliver high-quality software that meets customer requirements.

Thanks for reading...
Happy Coding!

Once unpublished, all posts by folasayosamuel will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If folasayosamuel is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag folasayosamuel:

Make all posts by folasayosamuel less visible

Report other inappropriate conduct

item.title: 用于生成自定义虚拟 JSON 并将其部署为端点的 Web 应用程序 | A Web App to generate custom dummy JSON and deploy it as an endpoint
item.description:
依赖者 Sethi

发布于 2 月 27 日

大家好，我很高兴分享我最近开发的一个 Web 应用程序，它可以在几秒钟内使用 AI 生成虚拟 JSON。 该应用程序还允许用户将生成的 JSON 部署到端点。

附带一提，我能够在不到一周的时间内开发出整个应用程序，包括规划、线框图、UI 设计、前端、后端和测试。 这是一个很大的挑战，但我对最终结果很满意。

我很想听听您对此应用程序的反馈，并且我愿意接受您可能提出的任何建议或改进。 所以请务必查看下面的 Web 应用程序链接，让我知道您的想法。
感谢您的支持！

与 REST 基于文本、基于 HTTP 的 API 样式相比，gRPC 提供了一种更高效的基于二进制的 API 样式，这更适合流式调用和大型主体。

如果 dependersethi 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

Depender Sethi

Hello everyone, I'm excited to share a web application that I developed recently which generates dummy JSON using AI in just a few seconds. The app also enables users to deploy the generated JSON to an endpoint.

On a side note, I was able to develop the entire application, including planning, wireframing, UI design, frontend, backend, and testing, in less than a week. It was quite a challenge, but I'm happy with the end result.

I would love to hear your feedback on this application, and I'm open to any suggestions or improvements that you may have. So please do check out the link to the web application below and let me know your thoughts.
Thank you for your support!

generatejson.com/

item.title: 实施正确的电子邮件验证流程 | Implementing the right Email Verification flow
item.description:
电子邮件验证是有争议的，它增加了摩擦并增加了入职的另一个步骤。 然而，如果做得好，电子邮件验证可以更好地与用户沟通，避免虚假账户/负面外部性，并保持较高的营销声誉。

在这篇文章中，我们将分析为什么电子邮件验证很重要，以及如何为您的应用程序实施正确的验证流程。

为什么我们需要电子邮件验证

与用户沟通

增加邮箱验证的主要原因是为了有一个直达用户的渠道。

公司电子邮件并不是最令人兴奋的话题。 我的收件箱里有一堆 AWS 营销电子邮件，积满灰尘。 我对 AWS re:Invent 或他们最新的产品发布并不感兴趣。 然而，很多年前，我是一名狂热的英雄联盟玩家，每当新的补丁说明进入我的收件箱时，我都会花几个小时阅读每一个变化。

公司内容在同一条船上。 创建引人入胜的内容是建立喜爱您产品的用户社区的最佳方式之一。 此外，公司通信还有很多有效用例，例如发送账单文件或密码重置说明。

避免假账户/负外部性

电子邮件验证有助于清除虚假帐户。 通常，虚假帐户旨在滥用应用程序的特定功能或产品。 这可能会导致服务器成本上升和数据分析出现偏差。 在电子邮件验证中增加额外的摩擦可能是降低产品滥用风险的低成本方法。

特别是，一些假账户可能附有真实的电子邮件地址。 攻击者可以使用电子邮件john@fbi.gov或susan@irs.gov注册。 你最不想做的就是用公司内容来骚扰约翰和苏珊。

账户关联

添加邮箱验证的另一个原因是为了防止攻击者在自动绑定账户时劫持账户。

例如，如果用户已有一个使用 Google 登录的帐户，则攻击者可以使用相同的电子邮件地址在 Email-password Sign-In 上注册。 今天的大多数系统会自动链接这两个帐户，因为它们共享相同的电子邮件地址并允许攻击者访问原始帐户。

为了解决这个问题，系统必须先验证电子邮件密码帐户，然后再将其链接到 Google 登录帐户。

营销口碑

如果产品已发货但用户没有发现它，它真的值得吗？

公司可以向用户发送电子邮件以庆祝功能更新、产品发布和案例研究。 但是，如果没有适当的电子邮件验证，公司最终可能会发送数百封被硬退回的电子邮件——电子邮件不存在。

电子邮件服务提供商 (ESP) 不会善待出现大量硬退回邮件的帐户。 他们会自动假定该帐户是垃圾邮件发送者并将该帐户列入黑名单。 这阻止了从公司更新到发送密码重置指令等更重要的操作的一切。

电子邮件验证操作指南

实施完美的电子邮件验证流程是摩擦和用户验证之间的微妙平衡。

通用验证

要问的第一个问题是，“通用电子邮件验证对我的申请有意义吗？”

在某些情况下，例如构建财务应用程序，让每个用户验证他们的电子邮件是至关重要的。 他们可能已经在期待详细的验证，并且有耐心采取额外的步骤来搜索他们的收件箱以完成验证。

另一方面，图像编辑等更琐碎的应用程序可能没有同样的奢侈。 大多数用户都希望尽快访问该产品，设置电子邮件验证障碍势必会导致转化率降低或流失率增加。

验证触发器

假设我们仍然想设置电子邮件验证 - 我们需要弄清楚哪些操作会触发电子邮件验证。 我们可以在用户注册后立即要求电子邮件验证，或者可能在用户返回进行第二次会话时要求电子邮件验证。

将入职摩擦从电子邮件验证转移到以后的时间可以使用户的过程更加自然。 例如，社交媒体平台可以最大限度地减少注册过程中的摩擦，以便用户可以立即开始消费内容。 稍后，当用户想要发布内容时，平台可以验证电子邮件以最大程度地减少垃圾邮件。

我们还可以使用条件语句来分割验证。

例如，如果有人使用 gmail.com 电子邮件注册，我们可以相对确定该电子邮件是合法的。 另一方面，tmmcv.net 电子邮件很可能是假的。

无论哪种方式，定义一个将启动电子邮件验证流程的触发器都是至关重要的。 启动后，流程应告知用户已发送验证电子邮件并锁定功能，直到用户确认该电子邮件。 如果用户没有动机去验证他们的电子邮件，他们将直接忽略验证电子邮件。

生成验证令牌
一旦电子邮件验证过程开始，第一步就是设计一个验证令牌。 使用 SuperTokens，验证令牌是随机的 128 个字符的字符串。 您可以生成任何随机的 64-128 个字符的令牌。 该长度可防止攻击者的暴力尝试。

生成令牌后，将为其分配 24 小时的到期时间。 与密码重置或电子邮件更改功能不同，电子邮件验证相对无害。 因此，一些公司甚至可能选择更长的验证时间。

现在我们已经完成了所有这些，是时候向用户发送电子邮件验证链接了。

这方面的一个例子是：

下面是发送验证邮件的伪代码：

 const crypto = require('crypto'); /* First, we need to generate a verification token. We're using the JSON Web Token library for our example. */ var token = crypto.randomBytes(128).toString('hex'); /* Here, we save the verification token in the db to be verified when the token is consumed */ db_startTransaction() { db_saveNewVerificationToken(userId, token); } /* Next, we use the token to generate a verification link. */ const emailVerificationLink = 'https://example.com/auth/verify-email?token=' + token; const mailConfigurations = { from: 'example@example.com', to: 'user@user.com', /* The text and subject can be personalized for your specific use case. */ subject: 'Verify your email to access Example', text: emailVerificationLink }; /* With all that done, it's time to send an verification email to our user */ sendMail(mailConfigurations);

在数据库中存储验证令牌

有时，用户可能会尝试请求多个电子邮件验证。 由于我们有足够的到期时间，我们可以在数据库中重复使用之前生成的令牌，并在用户每次请求新电子邮件时发送相同的链接。 如果到期时间到了，我们可以生成一个新的令牌并将新的链接发送给用户。 通常，这使我们更容易处理电子邮件验证逻辑，而无需创建和禁用多个验证令牌。

另一方面，如果我们想加强安全性，我们可以选择生成一个新的验证令牌，替换数据库中的验证令牌，并每次发送一个新的电子邮件验证链接。 这种方法的缺点主要在于处理以前生成的令牌。

在某些情况下，禁用它们是有意义的。 但是，这可能会让用户感到沮丧。 或者，我们可以启用所有令牌，并在使用其中一个令牌时禁用所有令牌。

将验证传递给后端

用户点击验证链接后，应该会向用户清楚地表明他们已经通过电子邮件验证。 这可以采用不同的登陆页面的形式，也可以是应用程序主页上的弹出窗口。

电子邮件验证链接的一种潜在边缘情况是，电子邮件客户端可能会出于扫描目的打开验证链接并使用令牌。 为防止这种情况，我们可以检查会话是否存在，如果不存在，则将链接重定向到带有验证按钮的页面。 电子邮件客户端不会单击该按钮，重定向到该页面的用户只需单击该按钮即可完成验证。

然后将 URL 中的令牌传递到后端进行验证。 在后端，一旦消费了验证令牌，用户的验证状态就应该改变。

 function verifyEmail(token) { /* First, we need to verify that the token corresponds with an actual user. */ rowFromDb = db.getRowThatContains(token) if (rowFromDb == null) { throw Error(“invalid password reset token”) } userId = rowFromDb.user_id /* Now we know that the user exists, so it is valid. We start a transaction to prevent race conditions. */ db_startTransaction() { /* Here we check if the user is already verified. */ if (userId.verified = 1) { db_rollback(); throw Error(“User is already verified.”); } /* Here we check if the current token has expired or not. */ if (userId.token_expiry < time_now()) { db_rollback(); throw Error(“Token has expired. Please try again.”); } /* Here we'll change verifications status to verified. */ db_saveNewVerified(userId, 1); db_commitTransaction(); }

结论

电子邮件验证只是整个入职流程的一小部分，但却有着惊人的细微差别。 执行不力可能会导致沮丧的用户流失。 虽然一开始你会侥幸逃脱，但从长远来看，忽视这一点是个坏主意。



原文:

Email verification is contentious, it increases friction and adds yet another step to onboarding. However, when done right, email verification can lead to better communication with users, avoid fake accounts / negative externalities, and maintain high marketing reputation.

In this post, we’ll break down why email verification is important and how to implement the right verification flow for your application.

Why we need email verification

Communicating with users

The main reason for adding email verification is to have a direct channel to users.

Company emails aren’t the most exciting topic. I have a stack of AWS marketing emails collecting dust in my inbox. I’m simply not excited about AWS re:Invent or their latest product launch. However, many years ago, I was an avid League of Legends player and whenever new patch notes hit my inbox, I would spend hours reading through every change.

Company content is in the same boat. Creating engaging content is one of the best ways to build a community of users that love your product. Plus, there are plenty of valid use cases for company communication, such as sending billing documents or password reset instructions.

Avoiding fake accounts / negative externalities

Email verification helps weed out fake accounts. Generally, fake accounts are meant to abuse a certain feature or product of the application. This can lead to rising server costs and skew data analytics. Adding in the additional friction in email verification can be a low-cost way of reducing the risk for product misuse.

In particular, some fake accounts might have real email addresses attached. An attacker could sign up with the email john@fbi.gov or susan@irs.gov. The last thing you want to do is badger John and Susan with company content.

Account linking

Another reason to add email verification is to prevent attackers from hijacking accounts during automatic account linking.

For example, if a user already has an account using Google Sign-In, an attacker can sign up with the same email on Email-password Sign-In. Most systems today will automatically link the two accounts since they share the same email address and give the attacker access to the original account.

To solve this, the system must first verify the email-password account before linking it to the Google Sign-In account.

Marketing reputation

If a product is shipped but users don’t discover it, was it really worth it?

Companies can email users to celebrate feature updates, product launches, and case studies. But without proper email verification, companies can end up sending hundreds of emails that hard bounce - the email doesn’t exist.

Email Service Providers (ESPs) don’t take kindly to accounts that see high levels of hard bounces. They automatically assume that the account is a spammer and blacklist the account. That blocks everything from company updates to more vital operations like sending password reset instructions.

A how-to guide on email verification

Implementing a perfect email verification flow is a delicate balance between friction and user verification.

Universal verification

The first question to ask is, “Does universal email verification make sense for my application?”

In some cases, like building a financial application, having every user verify their email is critical. They’re likely already expecting detailed verification and have the patience to take the extra step of hunting through their inbox to complete verification.

On the other hand, more trivial applications like image editing, might not have the same luxury. Most users are looking to access the product as quickly as possible, and throwing up an email verification barrier is bound to result in lower conversions or increased churn.

Verification triggers

Say we still want to set up email verification - we’ll need to figure out what actions trigger an email verification. We could require email verification as soon as a user signs up, or perhaps when the user comes back for the second session.

Shifting the onboarding friction from email verification to a later time can make the process much more natural for users. For example, a social media platform can minimize friction during the sign up process so that a user can immediately start to consume content. Later, when the user wants to post content, the platform can verify emails to minimize spam.

We can also segment verification by using conditionals.

For example, if someone is signing up with a gmail.com email, we can be relatively sure that the email is legit. On the other hand, a tmmcv.net email has a high chance of being fake.

Either way, it’s critical to define a trigger that will initiate the email verification flow. Once initiated, the flow should tell users that a verification email has been sent and lock features until the user has confirmed the email. If the user has no incentive to verify their email, they’ll simply ignore the verification email.

Generating verification tokens
Once the email verification process starts, the first step is to design a verification token. With SuperTokens, the verification token is a random 128 character string. You can generate any random 64-128 character token. The length prevents brute force attempts from attackers.

Once the token is generated, it’s assigned an expiration time of 24 hours. Unlike password reset or email change functions, email verification is relatively harmless. As a result, some companies may even opt for longer verification times.

Now that we’ve done all that, it’s time to send the user an email verification link.

An example of this is:

example.com/auth/verify-email?token=OTJ…

Here is pseudocode for sending a verification email:


const crypto = require('crypto'); /* First, we need to generate a verification token. We're using the JSON Web Token library for our example.
*/
var token = crypto.randomBytes(128).toString('hex'); /* Here, we save the verification token in the db to be verified when the token is consumed
*/
db_startTransaction() { db_saveNewVerificationToken(userId, token);
} /* Next, we use the token to generate a verification link. */
const emailVerificationLink = 'https://example.com/auth/verify-email?token=' + token; const mailConfigurations = { from: 'example@example.com', to: 'user@user.com', /* The text and subject can be personalized for your specific use case. */ subject: 'Verify your email to access Example', text: emailVerificationLink
}; /* With all that done, it's time to send an verification email to our user
*/
sendMail(mailConfigurations);

Storing verification tokens in the database

Occasionally, a user might try to request multiple email verifications. Since we have a generous expiration time, we can reuse the previously generated token in the database and send the same link each time the user requests a new email. If the expiration time is up, we can generate a new token and send the new link to the user. Generally, this makes it easier for us to handle email verification logic without having to create and disable multiple verification tokens.

On the other hand, if we wanted to tighten security, we could choose to generate a new verification token, replace the verification token in the database, and send a new email verification link each time. The drawback to this approach is mainly in dealing with previously generated tokens.

In some cases, it makes sense to disable them. However, it could lead to frustrated users. Alternatively, we could keep all tokens enabled and disable all of them whenever one of the tokens is consumed.

Passing verification to the backend

After a user clicks on the verification link, there should be a clear indication for the user that they’ve been email-verified. This can either take the form of a distinct landing page or it could be a pop-up on the home page of the application.

One potential edge case with email verification links is that email clients might open the verification link for scanning purposes and consume the token. To prevent this, we can check if a session exists and if not, redirect the link to a page with a verify button. Email clients won’t click on the button and users that are redirected to this page can simply click on the button to finish their verification.

The token in the URL is then passed onto the backend for verification. On the backend, once a verification token has been consumed, the verification status for the user should be changed.


function verifyEmail(token) { /* First, we need to verify that the token corresponds with an actual user. */ rowFromDb = db.getRowThatContains(token) if (rowFromDb == null) { throw Error(“invalid password reset token”) } userId = rowFromDb.user_id /* Now we know that the user exists, so it is valid. We start a transaction to prevent race conditions. */ db_startTransaction() { /* Here we check if the user is already verified. */ if (userId.verified = 1) { db_rollback(); throw Error(“User is already verified.”); } /* Here we check if the current token has expired or not. */ if (userId.token_expiry < time_now()) { db_rollback(); throw Error(“Token has expired. Please try again.”); } /* Here we'll change verifications status to verified. */ db_saveNewVerified(userId, 1); db_commitTransaction(); }

Conclusion

Email verification is only a small part of the overall onboarding process, but one with a surprising amount of nuance. Poor implementation can lead to frustrated users who will churn out. While you’ll get away with it in the beginning, ignoring this is a bad idea in the long term.

item.title: Perl Weekly #605 - 试图拯救一种正在消失的语言 | Perl Weekly #605 - Trying to save a disappearing language
item.description:
最初发表于Perl Weekly 605

你好呀！

许多犹太人定居在奥斯曼帝国，并不断交流和改变他们的语言。 这就是创造我们称之为“Judeo-Espanyol”或现代名称“Ladino”的语言的原因。 这些犹太人通常被称为“Sephardic”犹太人，因为“Sepharad”在希伯来语中意为西班牙。 它最初是使用名为Rashi和Solitreo的古老希伯来字母书写的，但在过去 100 年左右，它被改用了拉丁字母。

不幸的是，由于各种原因（阿塔图尔克的土耳其现代化、大屠杀、移民到美国、以色列和其他地方），大多数剩余的拉迪诺语使用者转而使用当地语言，而今天只有几万名拉迪诺语母语者，大多数其中60岁以上。

有各种草根项目来振兴语言。 其中之一叫做Kantoniko 。 这是一本多语言词典，包含例句、音频、海报等。还有一些实验可以将这些材料用于教育目的，供想学习拉丁语的人使用。 该项目有一些用 Python 和 JavaScript 编写的开源代码。 在各种 Creative-Commons 许可下也有很多数据。

我知道 Python 不是你的菜，但整个项目对你们中的一些人来说可能很有趣，可能会看一看。 我用英语录制了一系列关于该网站以及如何为它做贡献的视频。

享受你的一周！

您的编辑：Gabor Szabo。

公告

每笔销售的一部分利润将捐给 TPRF

文章

第一版 SPVM::File::Temp 和 SPVM::File::Find

断言你的环境

当容器镜像四处传递时，有关所需 env 设置的信息肯定会丢失。 这里有一些解决方案：如何确保您拥有所需的环境变量和值？

移动捆绑

诱人的TTD

我将 TDD 视为您很少能达到的理想。 在新建项目中，我很少知道事情应该如何工作，所以我的测试编写落后于代码编写。 然而，通常在“一些”开发之​​后，我开始感到缺乏测试，然后我的注意力就会改变。

便携式（ish）随机（ish）数字生成器

它是关于向 Mojolicious 添加模板片段功能，以便与 htmx 一起使用。

测试

订购您的测试

一方面，我认为按顺序测试是个好主意，但另一方面，我们也希望确保测试的成功不取决于它们的顺序，并且它们也可以并行执行。 所以我不太赞成指定测试的顺序。

乐趣

一行：从多个文件中删除同一行

每周挑战

您甚至可以通过参加每周挑战赢取价值 50 美元的亚马逊代金券。 我们在月底从当月的所有贡献者中选出一名获胜者。 月度奖由PerlCareers的 Peter Sergeant 友情赞助。

每周挑战 - 206

欢迎来到新的一周，这里有几个有趣的任务“最短时间”和“阵列配对”。 如果您是每周挑战的新手，那么为什么不加入我们，每周都玩得开心。 欲了解更多信息，请阅读常见问题解答。

回顾 - 每周挑战 - 205

快速回顾上周 PWC 团队在 Perl 和 Raku 中处理“第三高”和“最大异或”任务的贡献。 你会发现很多解决方案让你忙个不停。

最高的最大值

干得好，继续保持。

一篇非常详尽的博客文章，有很多曲折。 干得好，感谢分享。

重置内容

不仅是标题，整个故事情节也不容错过。 强烈推荐。

经典的 Raku 权力展示。 对于任何寻找 Raku 魔术的人来说，这就是地方。

一如既往地用 Perl 制作精美的解决方案。 感谢您分享细节，不容忽视。

感谢分享。

干得好，继续保持。

对洛朗的期望不低。 感谢您的贡献。

急于嵌套循环！

做得好。

知道 Perl 中的这个单行代码。 这周他也没有让我们失望。 做得好。

每周挑战 205

两个任务的任务分析都写得很好，特别是第二个。 只是喜欢它。 谢谢。

简单的 for 循环是你本周的朋友。 罗比分享如何？ 感谢您分享知识。

罗杰从不错过使用语言这一强大工具的机会。 干得好，继续保持下去。

每周挑战 205

当谈到 Perl 时，使用 CPAN 会很方便。 顺便说一下，Python 也不甘落后。

每周收藏

上周发布了很棒的 CPAN 模块；
事件报告

您加入了 Perl Weekly 以获得有关 Perl 编程语言和相关主题的每周电子邮件。

想看更多？ 查看所有问题的存档。

还没有订阅时事通讯？ 免费加入我们！

文章版权归各自作者所有。



原文:

Originally published at Perl Weekly 605

Hi there!

In 1492 the Jews were expelled from Spain and 4 years later also from Portugal. Many of those Jews settled in the Ottoman Empire and kept talking and changing their language. This is what created the language we call 'Judeo-Espanyol' or in its modern name 'Ladino'. For 500 years these people kept speaking and improving their language which is now a mix of medieval Spanish, Portuguese, Catalan, Turkish, Greek, Italian, Hebrew, Arabic, and probably a few other languages. These Jews are usually referred to as the 'Sephardic' Jews because 'Sepharad' means Spain in Hebrew. It was originally written using old Hebrew letters called Rashi and Solitreo, but in the last 100 years or so it was switched to Latin letters.

Unfortunately due to various reasons (The modernization of Turkey by Atatürk, the Holocaust, emigration to USA, Israel and elsewhere) the majority of remaining Ladino speakers switched to the local language and today there are only a few ten thousands of native Ladino speakers, most of them above 60.

There are various grass-root projects to revitalize the language. One of them is called Kantoniko. It is a multi-lingual dictionary with sample sentences, audio, poster etc. There are also experiments to use the material for educational purposes for people who would like to learn Ladino. The project has some Open Source code written in Python and JavaScript. There is also a lot of data under various Creative-Commons licenses.

I know Python is not your cup of tea, but the whole project might be interesting to some of you and might take a look at it. I recorded a series of videos in English about the site and how to contribute to it.

Enjoy your week!

--
Your editor: Gabor Szabo.

Announcements

Perl and Raku Merch from Freewear

A portion of the profit from each sale goes to TPRF

Articles

First release of SPVM::File::Temp and SPVM::File::Find

SPVM (Static Perl Virtual Machine) is a perl-ish static typed programing language. SPVM provides fast calculation, fast array operations, easy C/C++ binding, and creating executable files.

Assert Your Environment

When container image gets passed around, the information about the required env settings will certainly get lost. Here is something of a solution to that: How to ensure you have the environment variables and values you need?

Data::Resolver base class for resolver

mobundle

Tantalizing TTD

I see TDD as an ideal that you can rarely attain. In green-field projects I rarely know how things should work so my test-writing lags behind the code-writing. However usually after 'some' development I start to feel the lack of tests and then my focus changes.

Portable(ish) Random(ish) Number Generator

Template fragments with Mojolicious

It is about adding template fragment functionality to Mojolicious, for use with htmx.

Testing

Ordering Your Tests

On one hand I think having the tests ordered can be a good idea, but on the other hand we will also want to make sure that the success of the tests does not depend on their order and that they can also be executed in parallel. So I am not really in favor of specifying the order of the test.

Fun

one-liner: remove the same line from many files

The Weekly Challenge

The Weekly Challenge by Mohammad Anwar will help you step out of your comfort-zone. You can even win prize money of $50 Amazon voucher by participating in the weekly challenge. We pick one winner at the end of the month from among all of the contributors during the month. The monthly prize is kindly sponsored by Peter Sergeant of PerlCareers.

The Weekly Challenge - 206

Welcome to a new week with a couple of fun tasks "Shortest Time" and "Array Pairings". If you are new to the weekly challenge then why not join us and have fun every week. For more information, please read the FAQ.

RECAP - The Weekly Challenge - 205

Enjoy a quick recap of last week's contributions by Team PWC dealing with the "Third Highest" and "Maximum XOR" tasks in Perl and Raku. You will find plenty of solutions to keep you busy.

The Highest Maximum

Yet another power packed solutions in Raku. Well done, keep it up.

Exclusive Third Or First

A very thorough blog post with lots of twist and turns. Great job and thanks for sharing.

Reset Content

Not just the title but the whole story plot is not to be missed. Highly Recommended.

Perl Weekly Challenge: Week 205

Classic Raku show of power. For anyone looking for Raku magic then this is the place.

The Weekly Challenge 205

Beautifully crafted solutions in Perl as always. Thanks for sharing the nitty-gritty, hard to ignore.

PWC205 - Third Highest

Straight forward solutions in Perl and Raku is shared week after week without any fuss. Thanks for sharing.

PWC205 - Maximum XOR

Raku magical power can make any solutions look elegant. Great work, keep it up.

Perl Weekly Challenge 205: Third Highest and Maximum (Bit-Wise) XOR

Compact one-liner (kind of) in both Perl and Raku. Nothing less was expected from Laurent. Thanks for your contributions.

nested loops in a rush!

Raku is playing the smart game and getting the job done without any trouble. Great job.

Perl Weekly Challenge 205

Knows for this one-liner in Perl. He didn't disappoint us this week too. Well done.

Weekly challenge 205

Task analysis of both tasks is well drafted, specially the second one. Just loved it. Thanks.

The Weekly Challenge #205

Simple for-loops is your friend this week. Robbie shared how? Thanks for sharing the knowledge.

Third Exclusive

Roger never misses the opportunity to use the power tool of the language. Well done and keep it up.

Weekly Challenge 205

Use of CPAN can be handy when it comes to Perl. By the way Python is not far behind either.

Weekly collections

NICEPERL's lists

Great CPAN modules released last week;
MetaCPAN weekly report;
StackOverflow Perl report.

Event reports

FOSDEM 2023
